goal: Record non-Sentinel local imagery or classified-layer alternatives after the low-cloud Sentinel route-window candidates remained cloud/shadow blocked.

findings.summary: The archived selected SNIT node layer lists advertise WMS/WFS vector or classified layers, but no direct Pacuare-ready orthophoto or imagery-tile service is attached in the current source slice.
findings.implication: Do not replace the Pacuare source drape from the archived SNIT slice. Continue official orthophoto catalog search, first-party capture, or licensed aerial procurement.

findings.source_node: Sistema Nacional de Areas de Conservacion (SINAC)
findings.lineage_summary: Reference layer for 2023 forest-cover mapping from the National Forest Inventory context, using a machine-learning model over Sentinel-1 and Sentinel-2 with cloud-free-year classification and a 0.5 hectare minimum mapping unit.

rights_flags.other_restrictions: Para cualquier trabajo relacionado, hacer referencia a la fuente. SINAC, 2021 "Mapa de Bosques y Otras Coberturas 2021".

blocked_use: packaged production mask without terms review

findings.summary: The wetlands metadata lineage mentions 2005 official cartography and orthophotos plus high-resolution imagery used in some national coverage work, but the archived distribution is a vector WMS layer rather than an orthophoto service.
findings.implication: Use this as a lead for locating official Costa Rica orthophoto archives, not as an importable Pacuare imagery source.

recommended_next_steps: Search official Costa Rica cartography or orthophoto archive access outside the currently archived SNIT node slice.
If SINAC forest-cover terms permit, import only as a classified mask candidate and compare against guide-reviewed banks and canopy density.
Keep pursuing first-party or permissioned aerial/field capture for Pacuare photoreal rainforest detail.
